test_plan.strategy: Unit tests cover snapshot-reader projection logic and degradation branching. Integration tests cover the collector cycle end-to-end with a real spawned `aitri status --json` against a fixture project. E2E tests (Playwright) cover the rendered ProjectCard at 375px and 1440px and the FR-017 degradation warning row. Test fixtures construct projects under os.tmpdir() — no machine-absolute paths, no shared state.

type_coverage_matrix.rationale: logic — spawn + parse is integration-critical; unit covers projection edge cases
logic — projection equivalence with legacy is unit-testable; integration validates real cycle
UX — render contract for NEXT ACTION row at multiple severities and viewports
UX — visibility rule + per-reason rendering must be observed in real DOM
UX — staleness indicators, single-line behavior at ≥768px, mobile wrap at 375px
UX — singular/plural and silence-on-zero behaviors must render correctly
UX — relative-time bucket logic is unit-testable; render is e2e
logic — degradation branching covered by unit; integration for real failure modes; e2e for warning row

test_cases.title: Snapshot reader spawns aitri status --json with cwd set to projectDir and parses successful output
preconditions: Mock child_process.spawn to return exit code 0 and stdout containing valid ProjectSnapshot JSON with snapshotVersion=1, project='demo', and a non-empty nextActions array
test_cases.given: projectDir = os.tmpdir() + '/aitri-fixture-001' (created by setup), spawn mock returns { code: 0, stdout: JSON.stringify({ snapshotVersion: 1, project: 'demo', nextActions: [{ priority: 1, command: 'aitri verify-run', reason: 'Phase 4 approved', severity: 'warn' }], health: { deployable: false, deployableReasons: [{ type: 'verify_not_passed', message: 'verify has not run' }] }, normalize: { state: null, method: null, baseRef: null, uncountedFiles: null }, audit: { exists: true, stalenessDays: 5 }, bugs: { total: 0, open: 0, blocking: 0 } }) }
test_cases.then: spawn was called once with args ['aitri', ['status', '--json'], { cwd: projectDir, shell: false, signal: AbortSignal.* }]; result === { ok: true, snapshot: { snapshotVersion: 1, project: 'demo', ...as injected }, durationMs: <number>≥0 }
steps: stub child_process.spawn to emit the prepared stdout and close with code 0
assert spawn arguments and returned object shape
test_cases.expected_result: result.ok === true; result.snapshot.snapshotVersion === 1; result.snapshot.project === 'demo'; spawn was called with cwd=projectDir, shell=false, command='aitri', args=['status','--json']

test_cases.title: Snapshot reader returns ok:false with reason='spawn_failed' when aitri exits non-zero
preconditions: Mock child_process.spawn to return exit code 1 and stderr 'not an aitri project'
test_cases.given: projectDir = os.tmpdir() + '/aitri-fixture-002' (an empty dir without .aitri), spawn mock returns { code: 1, stdout: '', stderr: 'not an aitri project' }
test_cases.then: result === { ok: false, reason: 'spawn_failed', detail: 'not an aitri project', durationMs: <number>≥0 }
steps: stub spawn to exit with code 1
test_cases.expected_result: result.ok === false; result.reason === 'spawn_failed'; result.detail contains 'not an aitri project'; no exception thrown

test_cases.title: Snapshot reader kills spawn after 3000ms and returns reason='timeout'
preconditions: Mock child_process.spawn to never emit close — simulating a hung CLI
test_cases.given: projectDir = os.tmpdir() + '/aitri-fixture-003', spawn mock that never closes; opts.timeoutMs = 3000
test_cases.then: after ~3000ms, child.kill('SIGKILL') was called exactly once; result === { ok: false, reason: 'timeout', durationMs: ≥3000 && <4000 }
steps: stub spawn to return a child whose close event never fires
assert child.kill was called and result.reason === 'timeout'
test_cases.expected_result: result.ok === false; result.reason === 'timeout'; child.kill('SIGKILL') was called exactly once; durationMs in [3000, 4000)

test_cases.title: Snapshot collection per project completes within 500ms p95 across 100 runs on a fixture with 10 features
preconditions: Aitri Core CLI is installed on PATH (skip with notice if not)
Build a fixture project under os.tmpdir() with .aitri at v0.1.79+, 10 features under features/, populated 01_REQUIREMENTS.json + 03_TEST_CASES.json + 04_TEST_RESULTS.json + BUGS.json
test_cases.given: fixtureDir = await buildFixtureProject({ features: 10, populated: true })
test_cases.then: p95(durations) <= 500 (durations sorted ascending; index = floor(0.95 * 100) === 95)
test_cases.expected_result: durations[95] <= 500; if Aitri CLI not installed, test is SKIPPED (manual verification required: install aitri ≥0.1.77 and re-run)

test_cases.title: Collector cycle invokes snapshot-reader in parallel for all local projects
preconditions: 5 fixture projects under os.tmpdir(), each registered in a temp ~/.aitri-hub/projects.json
Spy on readSnapshot to record start/end timestamps
test_cases.given: 5 registered local projects, each fixture with .aitri at v0.1.79+
test_cases.then: readSnapshot was called exactly 5 times; the timestamp ranges overlap (max(start_i) < min(end_i) + 50ms — proves parallel, not serial); each call's cwd matches its project location
test_cases.expected_result: readSnapshot called exactly 5 times; max(startTimes) < min(endTimes) + 50ms (overlap proves parallelism); each invocation's cwd argument matches its registered project location exactly

test_cases.title: projectFromSnapshot returns aitriState shape equivalent to legacy aitri-reader output for the same .aitri
preconditions: Build a fixture .aitri with: approvedPhases=[1,2,3], currentPhase=4, verifyPassed=false, verifySummary={ passed:0, failed:0, total:0 }
test_cases.given: fixtureDir with the .aitri described; legacyState = readAitriState(fixtureDir); snapshot = await readSnapshot(fixtureDir).snapshot
test_cases.then: projected.aitriState.approvedPhases deep-equals legacyState.approvedPhases (both are [1,2,3] in some order); projected.aitriState.currentPhase === legacyState.currentPhase === 4; projected.aitriState.verifyPassed === legacyState.verifyPassed === false
steps: deep-equal compare on the shared subset of fields
test_cases.expected_result: Deep-equal on { approvedPhases, currentPhase, verifyPassed, verifySummary } between projected.aitriState and legacy aitriState

test_cases.title: Legacy 5 readers are NOT invoked on snapshot success path
preconditions: Spy on readAitriState, readRequirementsSummary, readComplianceSummary, readTestSummary, readBugsSummary
1 fixture project at v0.1.79+ that produces a successful snapshot
test_cases.given: fixtureDir produces ok snapshot; all 5 spies installed
test_cases.then: Each of the 5 spies was called exactly 0 times; readGitMeta, readAppVersion, readSpecQuality, readExternalSignals, readFeaturePipelines were each called exactly 1 time (unchanged readers)
test_cases.expected_result: demoted readers: callCount === 0 each; unchanged readers: callCount === 1 each

test_cases.title: Net line reduction in snapshot-active code path of lib/collector/ is ≥400 lines (excludes 5 demoted reader files)
preconditions: Pre-feature commit SHA stored as PRE_SHA in fixture
Post-feature commit is HEAD
test_cases.given: PRE_SHA = the parent of the first commit on this feature branch
test_cases.then: (deletions - insertions) parsed from the final summary line of `diff` is ≥ 400
steps: execSync the git diff command above
parse the summary line for insertions and deletions
test_cases.expected_result: Parsed (deletions - insertions) >= 400 from git diff --stat output; if not met, the test fails with the exact delta and the diff output is logged for review

test_cases.title: TC-012h: NEXT ACTION row renders command and reason for warn-severity action at 1440px
preconditions: Hub web running at http://localhost:3000
Stub dashboard.json with one project whose nextActions[0] = { command: 'aitri verify-run', reason: 'Phase 4 approved — run verify next', severity: 'warn' }
test_cases.given: viewport set to 1440x900; dashboard.json contains one project as described
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="next-action-command"]').textContent() === 'aitri verify-run'; page.locator('[data-testid="next-action-reason"]').textContent() === 'Phase 4 approved — run verify next'; the row has class containing 'severity-warn' OR computed background-color matches token #FDB022 (dark) / #B54708 (light)
steps: assert text content of next-action-command and next-action-reason locators
assert severity class or computed style
test_cases.expected_result: command text exactly 'aitri verify-run'; reason text exactly 'Phase 4 approved — run verify next'; severity-warn styling applied (class match or computed token match)

test_cases.title: TC-012f: NEXT ACTION row shows 'No action — project idle' with neutral styling when nextActions is empty
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose nextActions = []
test_cases.given: dashboard.json contains one project with nextActions: []
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="next-action-row"]').textContent() === 'No action — project idle'; the row does NOT have class 'severity-warn' or 'severity-critical'; no badge element present (locator('[data-testid="next-action-badge"]').count() === 0)
steps: assert exact text and absence of severity classes/badge
test_cases.expected_result: text === 'No action — project idle'; no severity-warn/critical class; badge element count === 0

test_cases.title: TC-012e1: Long command renders on its own line with no horizontal scroll at 375px
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose nextActions[0].command = 'aitri feature run-phase a-very-long-feature-name-that-exceeds-card-width 4'
test_cases.given: viewport 375x812; dashboard.json with the long command above
test_cases.then: page.evaluate(() => document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= 375) === true (no horizontal page scroll); the command element wraps to its own line — its computed width <= card content width; reason text is on a separate line below the command
steps: measure computed widths of command and reason elements
test_cases.expected_result: documentElement.scrollWidth <= 375; command element occupies its own line (its parent's flex-direction is column or it has display:block); reason element bottom > command element top + command element height

test_cases.title: TC-012e2: NEXT ACTION row is positioned immediately above PIPELINE section in the DOM
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project with valid nextActions
test_cases.given: rendered card with NEXT ACTION row and PIPELINE section both present
test_cases.when: page.evaluate to read getBoundingClientRect of both
test_cases.then: nextActionRow.bottom <= pipelineSection.top + 24px (allowing for one section-gap of 16px); no other section's top is between nextActionRow.bottom and pipelineSection.top
steps: page.evaluate to get rects of [data-testid='next-action-row'] and [data-testid='pipeline-section'] and all sibling sections
test_cases.expected_result: next-action-row.bottom + 16px section gap === pipeline-section.top (within ±8px); no intervening section

test_cases.title: TC-013h: DEPLOY HEALTH section renders one row per deployableReason when not deployable
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose health = { deployable: false, deployableReasons: [{ type: 'verify_not_passed', message: 'verify has not run' }, { type: 'blocking_bugs', message: '2 critical bugs open' }, { type: 'drift', message: 'phase 3 drifted' }] }
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with 3 deployableReasons as above
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="deploy-health-row"]').count() === 3; the 3 rows contain texts 'verify has not run', '2 critical bugs open', 'phase 3 drifted' in that order; each row has a data-reason-type attribute matching one of the input type values
steps: assert text content of each in order
test_cases.expected_result: exactly 3 deploy-health-row elements; texts in order; data-reason-type attributes are 'verify_not_passed', 'blocking_bugs', 'drift'

test_cases.title: TC-013f: DEPLOY HEALTH section is hidden when deployable is true
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose health = { deployable: true, deployableReasons: [] }
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with deployable=true
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="deploy-health-section"]').count() === 0
steps: assert deploy-health-section element does not exist
test_cases.expected_result: deploy-health-section element count === 0; no DEPLOY HEALTH header text visible on the page

test_cases.title: TC-013e: Fallback row renders 'Project not deployable — reason unavailable' when deployable=false but reasons array is empty
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose health = { deployable: false, deployableReasons: [] }
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with deployable=false and empty reasons (CLI contract violation simulation)
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="deploy-health-row"]').count() === 1; the row's textContent === 'Project not deployable — reason unavailable'; the row has class 'severity-warn'
steps: assert single fallback row with exact text
test_cases.expected_result: exactly one deploy-health-row with text 'Project not deployable — reason unavailable' and severity-warn class

test_cases.title: TC-014h: QUALITY shows 'verify stale (18d)' when health.staleVerify contains root with days=18
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose health.staleVerify = [{ scope: 'root', days: 18 }]
test_cases.given: viewport 1440x900; dashboard.json as described
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="verify-stale-indicator"]').textContent() === 'verify stale (18d)'; the element has class 'severity-warn'
test_cases.expected_result: text === 'verify stale (18d)'; class contains 'severity-warn'

test_cases.title: TC-014f: No staleness indicator renders when neither verify nor audit is stale
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose health.staleVerify = [] and audit = { exists: true, stalenessDays: 5 }
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with both clean (verify recent, audit recent)
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="verify-stale-indicator"]').count() === 0 AND page.locator('[data-testid="audit-stale-indicator"]').count() === 0 AND page.locator('[data-testid="audit-missing-indicator"]').count() === 0
steps: assert all three indicator elements absent
test_cases.expected_result: all three indicator element counts === 0; no text containing 'stale' appears in the QUALITY section

test_cases.title: TC-014e1: 'audit missing' renders when audit.exists=false (NOT 'audit stale')
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose audit = { exists: false, stalenessDays: null }
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with audit.exists=false
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="audit-missing-indicator"]').textContent() === 'audit missing' AND page.locator('[data-testid="audit-stale-indicator"]').count() === 0
steps: assert audit-missing element present and audit-stale element absent
test_cases.expected_result: audit-missing-indicator text === 'audit missing'; audit-stale-indicator count === 0

test_cases.title: TC-014e2: At 768px viewport, indicator stays on a single line; at 375px it wraps below test-count
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose audit.stalenessDays = 73 and health.staleVerify = [{ scope: 'root', days: 18 }]
test_cases.given: stub as above; the test runs the same render at two viewports
test_cases.when: (1) setViewportSize(768,1024) → page.goto → measure y-coord of [data-testid='verify-stale-indicator']; (2) setViewportSize(375,812) → page.reload → re-measure
test_cases.then: At 768px: y-coord of verify-stale-indicator equals y-coord of test-count line (±2px) — same line; At 375px: y-coord of verify-stale-indicator > y-coord of test-count line by ≥16px — wrapped
test_cases.expected_result: At 768px: |verifyStale.y - testCount.y| <= 2; At 375px: verifyStale.y - testCount.y >= 16

test_cases.title: TC-015h: Plural row renders when normalize.uncountedFiles=3
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose normalize = { state: 'resolved', method: 'git', baseRef: 'abc123', uncountedFiles: 3 }
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with uncountedFiles=3
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="normalize-warning-row"]').textContent() === '3 files changed outside pipeline — run: aitri normalize'; the row has class 'severity-warn'
test_cases.expected_result: text === '3 files changed outside pipeline — run: aitri normalize'; class contains 'severity-warn'

test_cases.title: TC-015f: Row absent when normalize.uncountedFiles=0
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose normalize.uncountedFiles=0
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with uncountedFiles=0
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="normalize-warning-row"]').count() === 0
test_cases.expected_result: normalize-warning-row count === 0; no text containing 'files changed outside pipeline' anywhere on the page

test_cases.title: TC-015e1: Singular row renders when normalize.uncountedFiles=1
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with normalize.uncountedFiles=1
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with uncountedFiles=1
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="normalize-warning-row"]').textContent() === '1 file changed outside pipeline — run: aitri normalize' (singular 'file', no trailing 's')
test_cases.expected_result: text === '1 file changed outside pipeline — run: aitri normalize'

test_cases.title: TC-015e2: Row absent when normalize.uncountedFiles=null (no baseline / mtime / git failure)
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with normalize = { state: null, method: null, baseRef: null, uncountedFiles: null }
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with uncountedFiles=null
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="normalize-warning-row"]').count() === 0
test_cases.expected_result: normalize-warning-row count === 0

test_cases.title: TC-016h: lastSession line renders 'last: phase 3 approved by claude · 2 days ago' when at is 2 days before now
preconditions: Mock current time via the formatter's clock parameter
test_cases.given: lastSession = { event: 'phase 3 approved', agent: 'claude', at: '2026-04-15T14:00:00Z' }; now = '2026-04-17T14:00:00Z'
test_cases.then: returns the exact string 'last: phase 3 approved by claude · 2 days ago'
test_cases.expected_result: returned string === 'last: phase 3 approved by claude · 2 days ago' (exact)

test_cases.title: TC-016f: No last-session line renders when lastSession is undefined
preconditions: Stub dashboard.json with one project whose lastSession field is undefined (omitted from the JSON)
test_cases.given: dashboard.json without lastSession field
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="last-session-line"]').count() === 0
test_cases.expected_result: last-session-line count === 0

test_cases.title: TC-016e1: Relative time bucket boundaries — 59s→'just now', 60s→'1m ago', 59m→'59m ago', 60m→'1h ago', 23h→'23h ago', 24h→'1d ago', 6d→'6d ago', 7d→absolute date
test_cases.given: now = '2026-04-17T14:00:00Z'; cases = [ { delta:59_000, expect:'just now' }, { delta:60_000, expect:'1m ago' }, { delta:59*60_000, expect:'59m ago' }, { delta:60*60_000, expect:'1h ago' }, { delta:23*3600_000, expect:'23h ago' }, { delta:24*3600_000, expect:'1d ago' }, { delta:6*86400_000, expect:'6d ago' }, { delta:7*86400_000, expect:'Apr 10, 2026' } ]
test_cases.when: for each case: result = formatRelativeTime(new Date(now.getTime() - delta).toISOString(), now)
test_cases.then: each result === expect
test_cases.expected_result: all 8 cases pass; result strings match expected verbatim

test_cases.title: TC-017h: Card-level warning row 'Aitri CLI not installed — limited report' renders when spawn ENOENT and legacy data renders below
preconditions: Force degradationReason='not_installed' in dashboard.json stub for one project; ensure legacy fields (aitriState, testSummary) are populated as if collected via legacy path
test_cases.given: dashboard.json with project.degradationReason='not_installed' and populated legacy fields (aitriState.approvedPhases=[1,2], testSummary={ passed:5, failed:0, total:5 })
test_cases.then: page.locator('[data-testid="degradation-warning-row"]').textContent() === 'Aitri CLI not installed — limited report'; the warning row's bounding rect top is less than the next-action-row top OR less than the pipeline-section top (positioned at the very top of card body); the test-count line still renders 'Tests: 5/5'
steps: assert warning row text and position
test_cases.expected_result: warning row text === 'Aitri CLI not installed — limited report'; warning row is the first child of card-body; test-count text contains 'Tests: 5/5'

test_cases.title: TC-017f: One project's snapshot failure does not block or delay other projects by more than 100ms
preconditions: 5 fixture projects: 4 succeed, 1 has spawn that returns code=1 immediately (simulating instant failure)
test_cases.given: 5 registered projects; project[2] returns ok:false reason='spawn_failed'; the other 4 return ok:true
test_cases.when: t0 = Date.now(); await collectAll(projects); totalDurationMs = Date.now() - t0
test_cases.then: totalDurationMs <= max(individualSnapshotDurations) + 100; all 5 projects are present in the result with project[2].degradationReason === 'spawn_failed' and the other 4 with degradationReason === null
steps: build 5 fixtures with the failure injected on index 2
assert duration bound and per-project degradationReason
test_cases.expected_result: totalDurationMs <= 100 + max(individual snapshot durations); project[2].degradationReason === 'spawn_failed'; other 4 projects.degradationReason === null

test_cases.title: TC-017e1: Project at aitriVersion=0.1.74 takes legacy path with 'version too old' warning, never spawns aitri
preconditions: Build a fixture .aitri with aitriVersion='0.1.74'
Spy on readSnapshot
test_cases.given: fixture with aitriVersion='0.1.74' registered as a local project
test_cases.then: readSnapshot.callCount === 0; result.degradationReason === 'version_too_old'; result.aitriState is non-null (legacy reader populated it)
steps: assert no spawn occurred and degradation flag set
test_cases.expected_result: readSnapshot.callCount === 0; degradationReason === 'version_too_old'; aitriState !== null

test_cases.title: TC-017e2: Failure is logged to ~/.aitri-hub/logs/aitri-hub.log with project name, reason, durationMs
preconditions: Override hubDir() to use a temp directory; fixture project that triggers parse_failed (spawn returns malformed stdout)
test_cases.given: tempHubDir = os.tmpdir() + '/hub-fixture-XXX'; spawn mock emits non-JSON stdout
test_cases.then: the log file at tempHubDir + '/logs/aitri-hub.log' exists; its last line parsed as JSON has fields { ts: <ISO string>, project: <name>, reason: 'parse_failed', durationMs: <number> }
steps: read log file and assert structured line shape
test_cases.expected_result: log file exists; last line parses as JSON with required fields; reason === 'parse_failed'

test_cases.title: TC-S001: NEXT ACTION row empty state shows guidance text 'No action — project idle' (component-state requirement from UX spec §2.1)
preconditions: dashboard.json with one project, nextActions=[]
test_cases.given: idle project as above
test_cases.then: next-action-row is visible AND its text content === 'No action — project idle' (NOT a blank area)
test_cases.expected_result: row visible and shows guidance text 'No action — project idle'

test_cases.title: TC-S002: NEXT ACTION row loading state shows skeleton during initial collection cycle
preconditions: Stub dashboard endpoint to delay first response by 500ms
test_cases.given: Hub web with delayed dashboard.json response
test_cases.then: skeleton element is visible at t<200ms; after dashboard.json resolves, skeleton is replaced by next-action-row
steps: assert skeleton gone and row present
test_cases.expected_result: skeleton visible during load; replaced by row after data arrives

test_cases.title: TC-S003: DEPLOY HEALTH section empty state — section is absent (no 'all clear' badge per minimalist principle in UX spec)
preconditions: dashboard.json with deployable=true
test_cases.given: deployable project
test_cases.then: deploy-health-section count === 0; no text 'DEPLOY HEALTH' anywhere
test_cases.expected_result: section count === 0

test_cases.title: TC-DT001: severity-warn token contrast ratio against card background ≥4.5:1 in both light and dark themes
preconditions: Hub web running; toggleable theme via [data-testid='theme-toggle']
test_cases.given: stub project with severity=warn next action; both themes available
test_cases.when: for theme in [light, dark]: setTheme(theme); load page; getComputedStyle on next-action-badge for color, on card root for background; compute WCAG contrast ratio
test_cases.then: contrast >= 4.5 in both themes
test_cases.expected_result: contrast(light) >= 4.5 AND contrast(dark) >= 4.5 (computed via WCAG luminance formula)
